data.title: Bitcoin Enthusiasts Gather in Las Vegas Amid Market Stagnation
data.description: Each year, dedicated Bitcoin enthusiasts gather, donning orange cowboy hats, ties, and T-shirts, to express their optimism for $1 million Bitcoin valuations. According to Bloomberg, this year's event took place in Las Vegas, where the enthusiasm was palpable among attendees. However, despite the fervent chants and high spirits, the market remained unaffected by the gathering.

data.title: Walrus Introduces MemWal for AI Agents on Sui-Based Protocol
data.description: Walrus (WAL) has launched MemWal, a memory layer and software development kit aimed at enhancing AI agents on its Sui-based storage protocol. According to NS3.AI, the product is designed to ensure that agent memory is verifiable, portable, and shareable across different AI models. Mysten Labs product manager Abinhav Garg highlighted these features as key benefits of the new offering.

data.title: Pentagon's 2027 Budget Proposal Excludes New Ukraine Military Aid Funding
data.description: Acting Inspector General Hearst announced that the Pentagon's budget proposal for fiscal year 2027 does not allocate new funds for military assistance to Ukraine. According to NS3.AI, Hearst informed a Senate hearing that this exclusion pertains to the Ukraine Security Assistance Initiative.

data.title: Illinois Investigation Reveals Misconduct by Federal Agents in Chicago
data.description: An Illinois state investigation has uncovered that officials from the Trump administration facilitated misconduct by federal immigration and border patrol agents during an enforcement crackdown in Chicago last year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the findings of the investigation, which point to a series of actions that allegedly violated protocols and standards. The investigation sheds light on the practices employed by federal agents under the directive of the Trump administration, raising concerns about the adherence to legal and ethical guidelines during the enforcement operations. The report calls for accountability and measures to prevent future occurrences of such misconduct.

data.title: Ether's Potential Move Toward $3,000 in May
data.description: According to NS3.AI, technical and on-chain analysis suggests that Ether may approach the $3,000 mark in May. The analysis highlights key resistance levels between $2,350 and $2,400, alongside support zones ranging from $1,980 to $2,178. Glassnode data indicates that 7.4 million ETH was acquired within these support levels. Additionally, CryptoQuant reports a positive spot taker CVD, with taker buy volume exceeding $1 billion, contributing to the bullish outlook.

data.title: Sentora Launches Smart Yield with $2 Billion Allocated Across Public Vaults
data.description: Sentora has introduced its Smart Yield platform for public users, with nearly $2 billion allocated across its public vaults. According to NS3.AI, the platform allows users to compare vaults based on asset, chain, strategy, APY, and risk metrics through a transparent, non-custodial interface.

data.title: AI TRENDS | U.S. Defense Secretary: AI Has Not Made Fatal Decisions; Anthropic Led by 'Ideological Zealot'
data.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated that artificial intelligence has not been responsible for making fatal decisions. According to Jin10, Hegseth emphasized the importance of human oversight in AI applications, particularly in defense scenarios. He also commented on Anthropic, describing its leadership as driven by ideological zeal. Anthropic, a company known for its work in AI safety and research, has been under scrutiny for its approach to AI development. The remarks highlight ongoing debates about the role of AI in critical decision-making processes and the influence of leadership in tech companies.

data.title: U.S. House Approves DHS Funding Bill to End Longest Government Shutdown
data.description: The U.S. House of Representatives passed a funding bill for the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) on May 1, effectively ending a 76-day partial government shutdown. According to NS3.AI, the Senate had previously approved the measure, which now awaits the signature of U.S. President Donald Trump at the White House. The shutdown, which began on February 14, is noted as the longest in U.S. history.

data.title: Congress Approves Bill to Reopen Key Parts of Homeland Security
data.description: Congress has voted to reopen essential sections of the Department of Homeland Security following weeks of internal disputes among GOP members. CNN Breaking News posted on X, the bill is now on its way to U.S. President Donald Trump for approval. The legislative move comes after prolonged negotiations and disagreements within the Republican Party, highlighting the challenges in reaching consensus on national security matters. The reopening of these critical components is expected to restore operations and ensure the department can effectively carry out its duties.

data.title: US Dollar Faces Significant Monthly Decline Amid Iran Peace Talks
data.description: The US dollar is experiencing its most substantial monthly decline since June, driven by the anticipation of peace talks aimed at resolving the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X that traders are unwinding their positions on the dollar, traditionally seen as a safe-haven currency, in response to the potential diplomatic resolution. The prospect of peace has shifted market sentiment, leading to a reassessment of currency strategies among investors. As discussions progress, the dollar's performance continues to be closely monitored by financial analysts and traders.

data.title: Circle Launches Gas-Free USDC Nanopayments on Mainnet Across 11 Blockchains
data.description: Circle has introduced gas-free USDC Nanopayments on the mainnet, spanning 11 blockchains. This initiative is aimed at AI agents, APIs, and machine-driven commerce. According to NS3.AI, Circle stated that the system facilitates transfers as small as $0.000001, offering instant verification and batched on-chain settlement. The Nanopayments system expands x402 flows, which have processed over $100 million since their launch earlier this year.

data.title: U.S. Senate Bans Participation in Prediction Markets Amid Insider Trading Concerns
data.description: The U.S. Senate has unanimously approved a rule prohibiting its members from engaging in prediction markets, effective immediately as of April 30. According to NS3.AI, this decision comes in response to growing concerns about insider trading on platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket, as well as event contracts linked to death and violence. The rule aims to address ethical issues and maintain the integrity of legislative processes.

data.title: Analysts Maintain Bullish Outlook on Apple Ahead of Earnings
data.description: Goldman Sachs, Bank of America, JPMorgan, and Wedbush have maintained their optimistic views on Apple as the company approaches its earnings announcement on Thursday. These firms have set price targets for Apple ranging from $325 to $350. In contrast, UBS has taken a neutral stance with a price target of $287. According to NS3.AI, analysts are anticipating Apple's fiscal Q2 revenue to be approximately $109.5 billion, with earnings per share (EPS) expected to be between $1.92 and $1.94. JPMorgan has noted that the upcoming earnings call might also focus on Apple's planned CEO transition set for September 1.

data.title: Microsoft Shares Decline Amid Revised Capital Expenditure Forecast
data.description: Microsoft shares experienced a decline following the company's announcement that its capital expenditures for calendar-year 2026 are projected to reach $190 billion, surpassing its previous AI investment outlook of $150 billion. According to NS3.AI, despite this adjustment, Microsoft exceeded Bloomberg's consensus expectations with third-quarter earnings per share of $4.27, based on revenue of $82.89 billion. Additionally, Microsoft reported that its AI business has achieved an annual revenue run rate of $37 billion, marking a 123% increase year over year.

data.title: Colombia's Inflation Expectations for 2026 Rise to 6.40%
data.description: Colombia's inflation expectations for the end of 2026 have increased, according to a Reuters survey. According to Jin10, the survey indicates that the anticipated inflation rate has risen to 6.40%, up from the previous forecast of 6.21%.

data.title: A US Senate subcommittee has supported an AI-driven child safety bill, which will impact tech giants.
data.description: A powerful U.S. Senate committee has supported legislation requiring OpenAI, Meta Platforms (META.O), and other artificial intelligence companies to block minors from using AI chatbots, responding to growing public concerns about the harm this rapidly spreading technology poses to children and teenagers. Republicans and Democrats on the ideologically polarized Senate Judiciary Committee broke party lines to unanimously support the bill. The bill would require AI companies to maintain strict age verification systems, prohibit AI companionship services for minors, and ban chatbots from sending harmful information to minors. (Jinshi)

data.title: Hospitals Brace for Financial Strain Amid Medicaid Cuts
data.description: Hospitals nationwide are preparing for significant financial challenges as the Trump administration plans extensive Medicaid reductions. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that despite the looming cuts, healthcare facilities are borrowing at unprecedented rates, marking the fastest pace in over ten years. This borrowing trend reflects hospitals' efforts to secure funding and maintain operations in anticipation of reduced government support. The financial strain is expected to impact hospital services and patient care, prompting concerns among healthcare providers and stakeholders. As the situation unfolds, hospitals are strategizing to mitigate the effects of these budgetary constraints.

data.title: Ethereum Foundation Opens Applications for Protocol Fellowship Cohort 7
data.description:
The Ethereum Foundation has announced that applications for the Ethereum Protocol Fellowship Cohort 7 (EPF7) are now open, with a deadline of May 13. According to Odaily, the program aims to train engineers to participate in the development of Ethereum's core protocol, focusing on key network attributes such as censorship resistance, open-source nature, privacy, and security. It will emphasize client implementation, protocol specification, testing, and cutting-edge research.

EPF7 will adopt a 'small-scale, high-density' model, reducing the number of participants to enhance mentorship depth and project contribution quality. The program will run from June to November, offering selected participants guidance from the Ethereum core developer community. Some participants will receive monthly stipends to focus on protocol development work. The project aims to deliver long-term contributors to the Ethereum core research team and foster substantial achievements in client development and protocol research.

The EPF team will host an online briefing on May 6 at 15:00 UTC to further explain project details and answer application-related questions.

data.title: France's Finance Ministry Maintains Growth Forecast for 2026
data.description: France's Finance Ministry has stated that there is no reason to revise its growth forecast. According to Jin10, the government projects a GDP growth of 0.9% for 2026.

data.title: Trump Administration Withdraws Nomination for U.S. Surgeon General
data.description: The Trump administration has decided to withdraw the nomination of wellness entrepreneur Casey Means for the position of U.S. Surgeon General, as confirmed by a White House official. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the unexpected move by the administration. The decision comes amid ongoing discussions and evaluations within the government regarding key health positions. Casey Means, known for her work in the wellness industry, was initially considered for the role to bring a fresh perspective to public health leadership. The withdrawal of her nomination marks a significant shift in the administration's approach to filling this critical position. Further details on the reasons behind this decision have not been disclosed by the White House.
